agent.name: Marcus Chen
agent.notable_actions: Processed duplicate charge refund immediately; escalated overdraft refund path (contingent on bank statement); explained root cause of wrong-date charge

resolution.summary: Duplicate charge of $29.99 refunded immediately (3-5 business days). Overdraft refund of $35 not yet processed — contingent on customer emailing bank statement to billing@streamfit.com. Root cause (wrong-date January charge) acknowledged but not compensated directly in-call.

key_moments.description: Customer opens with anger — double charge of $59.98 vs expected $29.99
key_moments.trigger: Financial impact immediately felt; tone starts confrontational

key_moments.description: Customer challenges 3-5 day refund timeline: 'You took the money instantly but it takes a week to give it back?'
key_moments.trigger: Perceived asymmetry between charge speed and refund speed

key_moments.description: Customer discloses overdraft fee from January wrong-date charge — $35 in bank fees
key_moments.trigger: Prior billing error with real financial consequence (overdraft)

key_moments.description: Agent offers direct $35 refund contingent on bank statement — customer accepts
key_moments.trigger: Concrete resolution path offered after initial credit-only offer rejected

key_moments.description: Customer accepts summary: 'Alright. I'll hold you to that.'
key_moments.trigger: Resolution path confirmed; tension reduced but skepticism remains

pain_points.description: System generated duplicate charge on February 8th — two identical charges of $29.99 at the same timestamp.
pain_points.verbatim_quote: I've got $59.98 taken out of my account when it should be $29.99.

pain_points.description: January charge posted on the 3rd instead of the 8th (billing date), causing a $35 overdraft bank fee.
pain_points.verbatim_quote: That cost me $35 in bank fees because your system charged me on the 3rd instead of the 8th.

pain_points.description: Customer on Basic plan is frustrated that desirable classes are locked behind Premium, undermining perceived value of current plan.
pain_points.verbatim_quote: half the good classes are Premium-only... it's getting hard to justify.

motivations.description: Customer joined for perceived value; two billing errors have eroded that value proposition significantly.

intent.primary: Resolve duplicate charge and seek compensation for overdraft bank fee caused by wrong-date billing error
intent.secondary: Signal dissatisfaction with Basic plan value; considering cancellation if billing issues persist

factors: Two billing errors in two consecutive months
Real financial harm (overdraft fee) from StreamFit system error
Expressed explicit cancellation consideration: 'getting close to just ditching it'
Basic plan perceived as poor value due to Premium content wall

signals: Mentioned Premium-only classes he cannot access
However, trust is currently low due to billing errors — upsell attempt would likely backfire now

action_items.action: Process $29.99 duplicate charge refund — initiated in-call
Process $35 overdraft refund upon receipt of customer bank statement at billing@streamfit.com — contingent
Escalate wrong-date billing error to engineering to confirm fix rollout applies to David Wright's account
Monitor account for cancellation signal — customer expressed intent to leave if billing issues recur

review_reasons: Overdraft refund is contingent on customer action (bank statement email) — follow-up required to confirm receipt and processing.
Customer expressed cancellation consideration — account at risk if second refund is not honoured.
